target.name: Vertical Semiconductor
target.founder: Joshua Perozek, Cynthia Liao, Tomas Palacios

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $11M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.2x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Vertical Semiconductor is a Seed-stage semiconductor design company founded by Joshua Perozek, Cynthia Liao, and Tomas Palacios that has raised $11M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 8 private and 3 public semiconductor companies, including Series A peers Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), Substrate ($1.0B), and Rain AI ($500M).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $28M. Peer-median analysis of the four cited Series A comps produces a median valuation of $1.5B, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. Public comparable anchoring cannot be applied because the target's revenue is not disclosed; the semiconductor sector median trades at 24.2x PS across ALAB, NVDA, and AMD, but lacks a revenue denominator for Vertical Semiconductor.

The two applicable methods triangulate to a range of $23M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $764M. This wide dispersion reflects the early-stage nature of the company relative to more mature Series A peers. Key uncertainties include the company's technical differentiation, revenue trajectory, and whether its profile aligns more with the lower or upper quartile of the peer set. Near-term funding milestones and product validation will materially narrow this range.
